steps.title: Locate the relevant mechanism in the passage
steps.content: The passage states that attaching glass beads lets the light "focus on the beads instead of the particles," which overcomes the risk of overheating and damage to the particles. The advantage is that particles avoid direct exposure to the beam's intense heat.

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'It decreases the time it takes for the optical tweezers to locate and capture the particles.': the passage says nothing about locating particles faster. No time benefit is mentioned. ✗
  • Choice B — 'It facilitates the maneuvering of particles without directly heating the particles themselves.': the passage says the light focuses on the beads instead of the particles, preventing overheating — particles are manipulated without being heated. ✓
  • Choice C — 'It allows researchers to use weaker light beams to manipulate particles.': the passage never says beam intensity changes when beads are used. ✗
  • Choice D — 'It adds a material to which particles can transfer any heat absorbed from the optical tweezers' light beam.': the passage says heat focuses on the beads instead of the particles, not that particles absorb heat and then transfer it. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
